title: Add background removal and face centering
body:
## Goal
Process the uploaded photo so the subject is centered and the background is removed automatically.

## Scope
- Connect the backend process route to the Python AI service.
- Run background removal on the uploaded image.
- Detect the face and center the crop for passport framing.
- Return a processed preview URL for the frontend.

## Acceptance criteria
- A processed photo is produced from a valid upload.
- The subject stays centered within the passport layout.
- The frontend can display the processed result immediately.

## Suggested follow-up branch
`feature/bg-remove-center`

title: Enforce passport compliance and size presets
body:
## Goal
Make the generated photo match country-specific passport rules before printing.

## Scope

- Check background color, head ratio, and safe margins.

## Acceptance criteria

- Compliance issues are surfaced before print generation.
- Rules are documented and easy to extend.

title: Upload a selfie directly from the browser
body:
## Goal
Let a user choose a camera photo or file upload and move it into the app flow with minimal friction.

## Scope

- Validate file size, type, and orientation before upload.

title: Add webcam capture as an alternative to file upload
body:
**Problem**
UploadBox.jsx currently only supports drag-and-drop and file-browser selection. For a passport-photo tool, a large number of users won't have a pre-existing photo that meets specs (plain background, correct framing) — they need to take one fresh, often on the spot at a kiosk or shared computer. Right now there's no way to do that without first using a separate camera app and then uploading the result.

**Proposed solution**
- Add a "Take Photo" option alongside the existing drag-and-drop area in UploadBox.jsx (or as a toggle/tab: Upload vs Use Camera).
- Use the navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() API to open a live camera preview in a modal or inline panel.
- Let the user capture a still frame (canvas snapshot from the video stream), preview it, and either retake or confirm.
- On confirm, convert the canvas output to a File/Blob and feed it into the same usePhotoUpload pipeline already used for uploaded files, so downstream validation/processing doesn't need to change.
- Handle and message the cases where camera permission is denied or no camera is available.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] "Take Photo" entry point added to UploadPage/UploadBox
- [ ] Live camera preview with capture button
- [ ] Captured frame shown for confirm/retake before proceeding
- [ ] Captured image enters the same validation + upload flow as a normal file upload
- [ ] Permission-denied / no-camera-found states handled with clear user feedback
- [ ] Feature degrades gracefully (hidden or disabled) on browsers/devices without camera API support

**Labels suggestion:** frontend, enhancement

title: Add a reusable Toast/Notification component for error and success feedback
body:
**Problem**
The app currently has no shared component for surfacing feedback to the user. Looking at frontend/src/components/, only UploadBox.jsx, PhotoPreview.jsx, and LoadingSpinner.jsx exist — there's no Toast.jsx, Alert.jsx, or equivalent. This means events like:
- file too large / wrong format (caught in utils/fileValidation.jsx)
- upload failure (hooks/usePhotoUpload.jsx)
- AI processing failure (hooks/useImageProcessor.jsx)
- successful upload/processing

currently have no consistent way to reach the user. Either they fail silently, or each component ends up inventing its own ad-hoc error UI.

**Proposed solution**
- Add a Toast.jsx component (and a lightweight ToastContext/useToast hook) that can be called from anywhere: showToast({ type: 'error' | 'success' | 'info', message }).
- Mount a single <ToastContainer /> at the app root (App.jsx or AppRoutes.jsx) so any page/hook can trigger a toast without prop-drilling.
- Wire it into the existing validation and upload/processing hooks so failures/successes actually show something instead of failing quietly.
- Auto-dismiss after a few seconds, dismissible manually, stacked if multiple fire.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Toast component created, styled consistently with the rest of the UI
- [ ] Global toast context/provider available app-wide
- [ ] Invalid file type/size in UploadBox triggers an error toast
- [ ] Failed API calls in usePhotoUpload/useImageProcessor trigger error toasts
- [ ] Successful upload triggers a success toast
- [ ] Component is accessible (role="alert" / aria-live="polite" for screen readers)

**Labels suggestion:** frontend, enhancement, good first issue

title: bug: frontend test suite has failing file validation and formatter assertions

expected true to be false

title: bug: python-ai-service tracks README files that collide on case-insensitive filesystems
body:
## Summary

The repository tracks both `python-ai-service/README.md` and `python-ai-service/readme.md`. These paths differ only by case, so they collide on case-insensitive filesystems such as Windows and many macOS setups.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the repository on Windows or another case-insensitive filesystem.
2. Observe the checkout warning from Git.

## Actual behavior

Git warns that the paths collide and only one file from the group is present in the working tree:

```text
warning: the following paths have collided ...
  'python-ai-service/README.md'
  'python-ai-service/readme.md'
```

## Expected behavior

The repository should avoid case-only path duplicates so all tracked files can be checked out consistently on supported contributor platforms.

## Suggested fix

Keep one README filename, merge any unique content, and remove the case-only duplicate from Git.

title: chore: remove generated Vite timestamp config files from frontend
body:
## Summary

The frontend directory contains generated Vite timestamp config files that appear to be temporary artifacts rather than source files:

- `frontend/vite.config.js.timestamp-1779688548429-703d9cfa5f822.mjs`
- `frontend/vite.config.js.timestamp-1779764902952-52da4ea2e799f.mjs`
- `frontend/vite.config.js.timestamp-1780321879038-f93cbd766453d.mjs`

## Why this matters

Generated timestamp config files add noise to the repository and can confuse contributors about which Vite config is canonical. They may also keep reappearing locally if the pattern is not ignored.

## Expected behavior

Only the canonical `frontend/vite.config.js` should be tracked unless there is a documented reason to keep generated timestamp files.

## Suggested fix

- Remove the generated `vite.config.js.timestamp-*.mjs` files.
- Add an ignore pattern such as `frontend/vite.config.js.timestamp-*.mjs` if the tooling may regenerate them.

title: bug: SettingsPage bypasses configured API base URL for session endpoints
body:
## Summary

`SettingsPage` calls session endpoints with raw relative `fetch('/api/...')` URLs instead of using the shared API client configured by `VITE_API_URL`. This breaks deployments where the frontend and backend are on different origins.

## Relevant code

`frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.jsx`:

```js
fetch('/api/auth/sessions')
fetch(`/api/auth/sessions/${sessionId}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
```

The rest of the app has a shared Axios client in `frontend/src/services/api.jsx` that uses `import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL`.

## Actual behavior

When the backend is configured through `VITE_API_URL`, the settings session requests still go to the frontend origin at `/api/auth/sessions` instead of the configured backend origin.

## Expected behavior

Settings session requests should use the same configured API base URL and credential behavior as the rest of the frontend services.

## Suggested fix

Use the shared `api` client from `frontend/src/services/api.jsx` for the session list and revoke calls, or centralize these calls in a small session service.

title: bug: backend Jest suite fails on ESM-only file-type dependency
body:
## Summary

The backend test suite fails before running assertions because Jest is not configured to handle the ESM-only `file-type` dependency used by the upload middleware.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the repository.
2. Run `npm ci` inside `backend`.
3. Run `npm test`.

## Actual behavior

The test suite fails during module parsing:

```text
SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module

backend/node_modules/file-type/source/index.js:5
import * as Token from 'token-types';
```

The failure is triggered through:

```text
backend/src/middleware/upload.middleware.js:4
import { fileTypeFromBuffer } from 'file-type';
```

## Expected behavior

`npm test` should run the backend tests successfully.

## Possible fixes

- Configure Jest for ESM support in this package.
- Transform the relevant ESM dependency.
- Switch the test runner/configuration to one that matches the package's ESM setup.

## Relevant code

- `backend/package.json` has `"type": "module"`.
- `backend/package.json` test script runs Jest.
- `backend/src/middleware/upload.middleware.js` imports `file-type`.

title: bug: root npm scripts fail because package workspaces are not declared
body:
## Summary

The root `package.json` defines `lint`, `test`, and `build` scripts using `npm run ... --workspaces --if-present`, but the package does not declare a `workspaces` field. As a result, the root quality scripts fail immediately instead of running frontend/backend checks.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the repository.
2. Run `npm ci` at the repository root.
3. Run one of the root scripts:
   - `npm run test`
   - `npm run build`
   - `npm run lint`

## Actual behavior

Each command fails with:

```text
npm error No workspaces found!
```

## Expected behavior

The root scripts should either:

- declare workspaces for `frontend` and `backend`, then run package scripts through npm workspaces, or
- avoid `--workspaces` and explicitly run the intended package commands.

## Relevant code

- `package.json` scripts use `--workspaces` on lines 8-10.
- The root package has no `workspaces` array/object.

title: Color code inconsistency across CSS files
body:
**Description:**
Many CSS files in the frontend use hardcoded color values instead of referencing the CSS custom properties (design tokens) defined in `frontend/src/index.css`. This creates inconsistencies where the same semantic color appears as different hex values across the codebase, making maintenance difficult and visual theming unpredictable.

**Examples of the problem:**
1. Primary brand color `#2563eb` is hardcoded in dozens of places instead of using `var(--color-primary)`
2. Text colors like `#0f172a`, `#475569`, `#64748b` are hardcoded instead of `var(--color-text)`, `var(--color-text-muted)`, `var(--color-text-light)`
3. Surface/background colors like `#ffffff`, `#000000`, `#f8fafc` are hardcoded instead of `var(--color-surface)`, `var(--color-bg)`
4. Border colors like `#e2e8f0` are hardcoded instead of `var(--color-border)`
5. Some files use colors like `#3b82f6`, `#ef4444`, `#0ea5a4` which don't exist in the design system at all
6. White text on buttons uses `color: white` or `color: #fff` instead of `var(--color-surface)`

**Files affected (35+ CSS files):**
- `frontend/src/App.css`
- `frontend/src/pages/HomePage.css`, `EditorPage.css`, `UploadPage.css`, `PhotoStudio.css`, `HistoryPage.css`, `AdminDashboard.css`, `PrivacyPage.css`, `TermsPage.css`, `NotFoundPage.css`, `PrintPreviewPage.css`
- `frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.css`, `Footer.css`
- `frontend/src/chatbot/styles/chatbot.css`
- `frontend/src/components/BackgroundSelector.jsx`
- `frontend/src/data/EditorPageData.jsx`
- And many more component CSS files

**Design tokens available** (defined in `frontend/src/index.css`):
- `--color-primary: #2563eb`
- `--color-primary-hover: #1d4ed8`
- `--color-primary-light: #eff6ff`
- `--color-secondary: #64748b`
- `--color-success: #16a34a`
- `--color-warning: #d97706`
- `--color-danger: #dc2626`
- `--color-bg: #f8fafc`
- `--color-surface: #ffffff`
- `--color-border: #e2e8f0`
- `--color-text: #0f172a`
- `--color-text-muted: #475569`
- `--color-text-light: #64748b`

**Expected behavior:**
All CSS files should reference these design tokens via `var(--color-*)` instead of hardcoding hex values. When a new color is needed, it should first be added to the token system in `index.css`.

title: [FEATURE]: Enhance AI Background Removal with Edge Refinement and Artifact Cleanup
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

Problem Statement

The current background removal workflow performs well for most images, but in certain cases the processed output may contain visible imperfections around the subject. Fine details such as hair strands, ears, shoulders, and clothing edges can appear rough or jagged. Additionally, artifacts like white halos, leftover background pixels, or minor shadows may remain, reducing the overall print quality of passport photos.

Since passport and ID photos require clean and professional-looking results, these imperfections can negatively impact the final output and user experience.

Proposed Solution

Improve the post-processing pipeline by introducing an AI-powered Background Quality Repair module that automatically refines the generated image after background removal.

The enhancement should focus on:

✨ Refine subject edges for smoother and more natural boundaries.
🪮 Preserve fine hair details during background extraction.
🧹 Remove white or colored halo artifacts around the subject.
🌫️ Clean up remaining background fragments and noise.
☁️ Detect and reduce unwanted shadows left after processing.
👔 Improve edge quality around clothing, shoulders, and accessories.
📷 Maintain high image quality suitable for printing without affecting facial features.

Expected Outcome
After background removal, the processed image should undergo an additional refinement step that produces:

Cleaner and smoother edges
Better preservation of hair and fine details
Minimal background artifacts
Improved overall visual quality
Professional print-ready passport photos

Benefits
Improves the visual quality of generated passport photos.
Produces cleaner outputs suitable for official documents.
Reduces the need for manual editing by photo studios.
Enhances user trust in AI-generated results.
Provides a more polished and professional user experience.

Possible Implementation

Apply an image matting or edge-refinement technique after segmentation.
Use morphological operations or alpha matting to smooth object boundaries.
Detect and remove halo artifacts around the foreground.
Perform lightweight shadow and noise cleanup before generating the final printable image.
Ensure the refinement step adds minimal processing time.


### Describe the solution you'd like

The feature should integrate seamlessly with the existing AI processing pipeline.
The implementation should prioritize maintaining fast processing while improving output quality.
Existing functionality should remain unaffected, with refinement applied automatically after successful background removal.

Please assign this issue to me under GSSoC. I'd be happy to work on implementing this enhancement.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

body:
### Description
Currently, our backend authentication uses stateless session cookies with no active storage tracking. This prevents security auditing of active logins, terminating suspicious sessions, or automatically cleaning up stale user histories on database connections.
### Proposed Solution
1. Introduce a mongoose-backed active Session model to track log-ins with User-Agent and IP audits.
2. Implement an automatic TTL (Time-To-Live) index on session models to let MongoDB handle expired sessions.
3. Expose active session auditing and termination routes under auth protection.
4. Update authentication middleware to validate cookies against active DB session entries.

title: Implement Interactive User Preferences Settings Panel and Active Session Viewer
body:
### Changes Proposed
- Created `SettingsPage.jsx` and `SettingsPage.css` containing editable preferences and active session revocation controls.
- Linked to the Settings panel directly from the main `Navbar.jsx`.
- Deleted `DelayedFallback.jsx` and refactored the loading indicator (`LoadingSpinner.jsx`) to accept a `delayMs` property.
- Registered `/settings` route lazy-loading configurations inside `AppRoutes.jsx`.

Closes #1015

title: ELUSoC_2026: Implement Interactive User Preferences Settings Panel and Active Session Viewer
body:
### Description
The application lacks a consolidated interface for editing profile details, switching themes, changing language preferences, and viewing active login sessions. Additionally, importing a separate lazy loading fallback wrapper causes unnecessary rendering loops.
### Proposed Solution
1. Create a dedicated Settings Page dashboard in the frontend.
2. Integrate active session tracking components to list and revoke sessions.
3. Integrate theme mode selections and general preference options.
4. Replace the separate `DelayedFallback` utility by supporting direct delays inside the primary `LoadingSpinner`.

title: bug: Footer/Main layout is not responsive on mobile viewports
body: Description: On smaller mobile viewports, the layout fails to adapt correctly. The main introduction text and the navigation columns ("Product", "Company", "Contact") are displayed side by side, resulting in extreme text compression, awkward wrapping, and poor readability. Steps to Reproduce: Open the website on a mobile device (or use DevTools responsive viewer set to dimensions like iPhone 12/13/14 or smaller). Scroll to the bottom/footer section. Screenshot: Expected Behavior: The layout should stack vertically on mobile screens. The "SnapPass AI" branding and description should take up the full width, with the "Product", "Company", and "Contact" link lists stacking neatly underneath it. Actual Behavior: Elements maintain a multi-column desktop-like grid layout, causing text to squish horizontally. Suggested Fix: Add a media query to switch the flex-direction or grid layout to a single column on mobile viewports.

title: Feat/multi format print layout
body:
Closes #1012 

### Changes Proposed
- Created `PrintLayoutSelector.jsx` and `PrintLayoutSelector.css` offering A4, US Letter, and 4x6 dimensions.
- Embedded the custom selector in `PrintPreviewPage.jsx` and updated state properties to forward the selection to our sheet generator.
- Added validation for `layout` parameters inside `print.controller.js`.
- Centralized validation rules by creating `validators.js` and deleting `passwordStrength.js`.

title: ELUSoC_2026: Support Multi-Format Printing Template Layout Configurations
body:
### Description
The printing sheet generator currently assumes a standard hardcoded A4 dimension format. Users printing on smaller cards or photo sheets (e.g. US Letter, 4"x6" photo cardstock) cannot resize layouts or select custom margin configurations.
### Proposed Solution
1. Create a custom template sheet picker element in the print preview stage.
2. Add layout selection parameters to the sheet generation route.
3. Consolidate client-side helpers into a unified validators library.

title: feat: add global scroll-to-top button across all pages, not just home page
body: ## 🔝 Add Global Scroll-to-Top Button Across All Pages ## Summary ### What does this PR do? Adds a global scroll-to-top button that appears on every page of the app. Previously, the `BackToTop` component only existed on the HomePage. This PR creates a new reusable `ScrollToTopButton` component, mounts it in `App.jsx` so it renders globally, and removes the old `BackToTop` from `HomePage.jsx` to avoid duplicates. body:
Bumps [framer-motion](https://github.com/motiondivision/motion) from 12.40.0 to 12.42.2.
Changelog

Sourced from framer-motion's changelog.

Changelog

Motion adheres to Semantic Versioning.

Undocumented APIs should be considered internal and may change without warning.

[12.42.1] 2026-06-30

Fixed

  • animateView: Old layer fade out now cancelled when defining .new().

[12.42.0] 2026-06-24

Changed

  • animateView: Layers are automatically grouped to match their DOM-hierarchy. New .group(false) method opts-out.

Fixed

  • animateView: Auto-crop is now aspect-ratio aware, disabling crops for matching aspect-ratios.
  • animateView: Disabled automatic border-radius animation.

[12.41.0] 2026-06-23

Added

  • animateView: Moves from Motion+ Early Access and alpha to main library.
  • animateView: .add() resolves a CSS selector or Element to automatically generate, apply and remove view-transition-name.
  • animateView: .new() and .old() configures values to animate on new and old layers.
  • animateView: .layout() can set a custom transition on the size/position animation of the currently selected elements.
  • animateView: Group layers now automatically crop with children set to cover, with border-radius animating from old radius to new. .crop(false) disables this behaviour.
  • animateView: .class(name) tags currently selected elements with a view-transition-class as a custom CSS hook.

Fixed

  • AnimatePresence: Prevent stuck exit animations when children interrupt.
  • drag: Child e.stopPropagation() no longer break drag end.
  • Fixing Next.js OOM on Windows when importing via motion package.
  • animateLayout: Improve handling of parallel/interleaved calls.

Changed

  • animateView: .enter() and .exit() now refer specifically to new and old layers where there are no matching old or new layers.
  • animateView: Interrupted transition setups now return resolved animation rather than throwing.

title: build(deps): bump actions/upload-artifact from 4 to 7
body:
Bumps [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) from 4 to 7.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in actions/upload-artifact#754

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/compare/v6...v7.0.0

v6.0.0

v6 - What's new

[!IMPORTANT] actions/upload-artifact@v6 now runs on Node.js 24 (runs.using: node24) and requires a minimum Actions Runner version of 2.327.1. If you are using self-hosted runners, ensure they are updated before upgrading.

Node.js 24

This release updates the runtime to Node.js 24. v5 had preliminary support for Node.js 24, however this action was by default still running on Node.js 20. Now this action by default will run on Node.js 24.

What's Changed

  • Upload Artifact Node 24 support by @​salmanmkc in actions/upload-artifact#719
  • fix: update @​actions/artifact for Node.js 24 punycode deprecation by @​salmanmkc in actions/upload-artifact#744

title: build(deps): bump actions/github-script from 7 to 9
body:
Bumps [actions/github-script](https://github.com/actions/github-script) from 7 to 9.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/github-script's releases.

v9.0.0

New features:

  • getOctokit factory function — Available directly in the script context. Create additional authenticated Octokit clients with different tokens for multi-token workflows, GitHub App tokens, and cross-org access. See Creating additional clients with getOctokit for details and examples.
  • Orchestration ID in user-agent — The ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID environment variable is automatically appended to the user-agent string for request tracing.

Breaking changes:

  • require('@actions/github') no longer works in scripts. The upgrade to @actions/github v9 (ESM-only) means require('@actions/github') will fail at runtime. If you previously used patterns like const { getOctokit } = require('@actions/github') to create secondary clients, use the new injected getOctokit function instead — it's available directly in the script context with no imports needed.
  • getOctokit is now an injected function parameter. Scripts that declare const getOctokit = ... or let getOctokit = ... will get a SyntaxError because JavaScript does not allow const/let redeclaration of function parameters. Use the injected getOctokit directly, or use var getOctokit = ... if you need to redeclare it.
  • If your script accesses other @actions/github internals beyond the standard github/octokit client, you may need to update those references for v9 compatibility.

What's Changed

  • Add ACTIONS_ORCHESTRATION_ID to user-agent string by @​Copilot in actions/github-script#695
  • ci: use deployment: false for integration test environments by @​salmanmkc in actions/github-script#712

title: build(deps): bump github/codeql-action from 3 to 4
body:
Bumps [github/codeql-action](https://github.com/github/codeql-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from github/codeql-action's releases.

v3.36.2

  • Cache CodeQL CLI version information across Actions steps. #3943
  • Reduce requests while waiting for analysis processing by using exponential backoff when polling SARIF processing status. #3937
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.6. #3948

v3.36.1

No user facing changes.

v3.36.0

  • Breaking change: Bump the minimum required CodeQL bundle version to 2.19.4. #3894
  • Add support for SHA-256 Git object IDs. #3893
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.5. #3926

v3.35.5

  • We have improved how the JavaScript bundles for the CodeQL Action are generated to avoid duplication across bundles and reduce the size of the repository by around 70%. This should have no effect on the runtime behaviour of the CodeQL Action. #3899
  • For performance and accuracy reasons, improved incremental analysis will now only be enabled on a pull request when diff-informed analysis is also enabled for that run. If diff-informed analysis is unavailable (for example, because the PR diff ranges could not be computed), the action will fall back to a full analysis. #3791
  • If multiple inputs are provided for the GitHub-internal analysis-kinds input, only code-scanning will be enabled. The analysis-kinds input is experimental, for GitHub-internal use only, and may change without notice at any time. #3892
  • Added an experimental change which, when running a Code Scanning analysis for a PR with improved incremental analysis enabled, prefers CodeQL CLI versions that have a cached overlay-base database for the configured languages. This speeds up analysis for a repository when there is not yet a cached overlay-base database for the latest CLI version. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in May. #3880

v3.35.4

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.4. #3881

v3.35.3

  • Upcoming breaking change: Add a deprecation warning for customers using CodeQL version 2.19.3 and earlier. These versions of CodeQL were discontinued on 9 April 2026 alongside GitHub Enterprise Server 3.15, and will be unsupported by the next minor release of the CodeQL Action. #3837
  • Configurations for private registries that use Cloudsmith or GCP OIDC are now accepted. #3850

title: ELUSoC_2026 Enhance Dependabot Configuration and Add Automated Security Scanning Pipelines
body:
# Problem
SnapPass AI's Dependabot configuration is minimal (3 ecosystems, no scheduling, no labeling, no grouping). There are no automated security scanning tools beyond Dependabot. No vulnerability scanning (Trivy), no SAST (Semgrep), and no npm audit automation exists.

# Current Behavior
- Dependabot only covers npm (frontend/backend) and pip (python-ai-service)
- No Docker or GitHub Actions ecosystem updates
- No schedule, assignees, reviewers, or group configuration
- No automated npm audit runs
- No Trivy filesystem scanning
- No Semgrep SAST analysis on PRs
- No OpenSSF Scorecard tracking

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Comprehensive security automation is critical for an open-source project handling user-uploaded images. Automated scanning catches vulnerabilities early, Dependabot grouping reduces PR noise, and SAST analysis prevents security regressions in PRs.

# Proposed Solution
- Rewrite dependabot.yml with 7 ecosystem entries, scheduling, groups, reviewers
- Create npm audit automation workflow with vulnerability issue creation
- Create Trivy filesystem vulnerability scanner workflow
- Create Semgrep SAST analysis workflow (per PR + weekly)
- Create OpenSSF Scorecard integration
- Update SECURITY.md with scanning tool reference
- Update README.md with security documentation

# Expected Outcome
- Dependabot covers npm, pip, Docker, GitHub Actions with organized schedules
- Weekly npm audit creates issues for high/critical vulnerabilities
- Trivy scans filesystem for CVEs on weekly schedule
- Semgrep runs SAST on every PR, uploading SARIF results
- Project's OpenSSF Scorecard is publicly tracked

title: ELUSoC_2026 Add Security.txt, Enhanced CSP, and Comprehensive Security Threat Model Documentation
body:
# Problem
SnapPass AI has several security gaps: no security.txt (RFC 9116) for vulnerability disclosure, no robots.txt for crawler control, incomplete CSP configuration, and thin threat model documentation. The SECURITY.md lacks in-scope/out-of-scope guidance.

- Add custom security headers middleware

- Security researchers can find disclosure channel via security.txt
- Crawlers are blocked from /api/ and /admin/
- CSP prevents clickjacking and form submission to untrusted origins
- Permissions-Policy restricts browser API access

title: [FEATURE]: Generate descriptive filenames for downloaded passport photo sheets
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

Currently, downloaded passport photo sheets are saved with a generic timestamp-based filename (e.g., `snappass_sheet_1719763200000.png`).

While this works functionally, the filename does not provide any context about the generated passport photo. Users who download multiple passport sheets find it difficult to identify or organize their files without opening each one individually.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Generate a more descriptive filename for downloaded passport photo sheets by including the selected passport size preset (or country, if available) along with the current date and time.

For example:

- `snappass_35x45_2026-06-30_17-45.png`
- `snappass_india_35x45_2026-06-30_17-45.png`

If the preset information is unavailable, the application can gracefully fall back to the existing timestamp-based filename.

This enhancement improves file organization while keeping the current download functionality unchanged.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

The current workaround is to manually rename downloaded files after each download. While this is possible, it becomes inconvenient when users generate multiple passport photo sheets in a single session.

### Additional Context

This is a frontend-only enhancement and is expected to require changes only in the download logic of the Print Preview page.

The current implementation saves files using a generic timestamp-based filename. Replacing it with a descriptive filename would improve the user experience without affecting the existing download workflow.

title: [FEATURE]: Country-Specific Passport & Visa Presets Catalog with Crop Overlays
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

Currently, when users upload a photo, they have to manually guess or search for the dimensions, crop margins, and background colors required by different government agencies (e.g., US Visa vs. Indian Passport vs. Schengen Visa). 

Since official requirements are extremely strict—with specific ratios for pupillary distance, face height coverage (70-80% in Schengen vs. 50-69% in US), and background luminance—a single generic crop frequently leads to visa application rejections. It is frustrating to manually crop and resize photos repeatedly without knowing if they meet the official rules of the target country.


### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like a country preset selection feature that handles official dimension guidelines. This can be implemented in phases:

1. Central configuration database (`presets.json`) listing country requirements (e.g., USA, India, Schengen, UK) with their target print sizes (in mm), head size percentages, and background color constraints.
2. Searchable dropdown in the React UI that lets users select a country/visa category.
3. Visual crop guide template (dashed oval lines indicating correct head/chin alignment) displayed on top of the image upload container.
4. Core backend integration so that the Python AI model auto-centers and crops images dynamically based on the specific selected preset dimensions.


### Describe alternatives you've considered

The only alternative is forcing users to search external government websites for passport specifications, calculate pixel dimensions manually, and use basic sliders to guess the crop boundaries, which is slow and prone to errors.


### Additional Context

This feature request is designed to align directly with GSSoC 2026 guidelines. Since it covers multiple difficulty levels, it can be easily assigned as:

- `gssoc:level1`: Creating the JSON database layout specs under a shared configuration folder.
- `gssoc:level2`: Building the searchable dropdown selector component and UI cropping overlay guidelines.
- `gssoc:level3`: Implementing dynamic Python backend scaling, auto-cropping ratios, and verification.

bug: python-ai-service has no main.py entrypoint — docker compose up fails immediately for the AI container, blocking all Phase 2 AI feature development
body:
## Summary

The `docker-compose.yml` explicitly documents:

> *"Note: the python-ai-service container expects a `main.py` entrypoint
> in `python-ai-service/`."*

However, `python-ai-service/` contains only:
python-ai-service/

└── app/

└── services/

├── bg_remove.py

├── face_center.py

├── dpi_optimizer.py

└── sheet_generator.py

There is **no `main.py`** at `python-ai-service/` root. Running
`docker compose up --build` causes the Python AI container to fail
immediately at the entrypoint with:
python: can't open file '/app/main.py': [Errno 2] No such file or directory

This kills the entire Docker Compose stack in some configurations, or leaves
the AI service in a crash-loop that silently breaks all backend calls to
`AI_SERVICE_URL=http://localhost:8000`.

## Impact

Every Phase 2 feature — background removal (`rembg`), face detection
(OpenCV), DPI optimisation, A4 sheet generation — depends on this
Flask service being reachable. Without `main.py`, zero AI processing
works even when all other services start correctly.

The README's "Docker (Compose)" section promises:

> *"Run all services (frontend, backend, python-ai-service, MongoDB) with
> one command: `docker compose up --build`"*

This promise is currently broken for the AI service.

## Proposed Fix

Create `python-ai-service/main.py` as the Flask application entrypoint
that registers all four service modules:

```python
# python-ai-service/main.py

from flask import Flask
from flask_cors import CORS

app = Flask(__name__)
CORS(app)

@app.route("/health")
def health():
    return {"status": "ok", "service": "snappass-ai-python"}, 200

@app.route("/process", methods=["POST"])
def process():
    # Wire to bg_remove → face_center → dpi_optimizer → sheet_generator
    return {"message": "AI pipeline not yet implemented"}, 200

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=8000, debug=False)
```

This stub makes `docker compose up --build` succeed and gives Phase 2
contributors a working entry point to implement the AI pipeline into.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `python-ai-service/main.py` created with Flask app and `/health` route
- [ ] `docker compose up --build` completes without error for all 4 services
- [ ] `GET http://localhost:8000/health` returns `{"status": "ok"}`
- [ ] `python-ai-service/requirements.txt` includes `flask` and `flask-cors`

**Labels:** `bug`, `python-ai-service`, `docker`, `level: beginner`

title: bug: frontend and backend both configured on port 3000 in README manual setup — simultaneous local dev impossible, VITE_API_URL points to wrong port causing all API calls to fail
body:
## Summary

The README manual setup instructions contain a critical port conflict that makes
it impossible to run the full stack locally without Docker. Additionally, the
frontend environment variable points to the wrong backend port, causing every
API call to fail silently on a standard developer setup.

## Exact Evidence

**README — "Run the Backend" section:**
> *"Backend runs at http://localhost:3000"*

**README — "Run the Frontend" section:**
> *"Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser"*

Both services are configured to bind port 3000 simultaneously. The backend
(`npm run dev`) will claim port 3000 first. The frontend Vite dev server will
fail to start with `Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::3000`.

**Frontend environment variable (from README):**
VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api

**Docker Compose (actual correct configuration):**
```yaml
frontend: ports: ["5173:5173"]
backend:  ports: ["5000:5000"]
```

The `docker-compose.yml` is correct — backend on 5000, frontend on 5173.
But the manual README instructions and `VITE_API_URL` both use 3000, directly
contradicting the Docker setup. A developer following the README without Docker
will have:

1. Port collision preventing either server from starting
2. `VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api` pointing at the frontend Vite dev
   server instead of the backend Express server — every `fetch` call returns
   HTML instead of JSON, causing silent parsing errors in every API response

## Reproduction

```bash
# Terminal 1
cd backend && npm run dev
# Backend starts on :3000

# Terminal 2
cd frontend && npm start
# CRASH: EADDRINUSE :3000
# OR if started first — all API calls hit :3000 (frontend) not :5000 (backend)
```

## Expected Fix

Update `README.md` manual setup section to match the Docker Compose port mapping:
Frontend: http://localhost:5173/    (Vite default)

Backend:  http://localhost:5000/    (Express on PORT=5000)

Update the Frontend env documentation:
VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:5000/api

Add a note clarifying that Docker Compose ports and manual dev ports differ,
and that contributors running manually must set `PORT=5000` in `backend/.env`.

**Labels:** `bug`, `documentation`, `good-first-issue`

title: build(deps): bump driver.js from 1.4.0 to 1.6.0 in /frontend

labels.name: javascript
labels.description: Pull requests that update javascript code

body:
Bumps [driver.js](https://github.com/nilbuild/driver.js) from 1.4.0 to 1.6.0.
Release notes

Sourced from driver.js's releases.

1.6.0

Added

  • animationDuration config to control how long the highlight transition takes. (#104)
  • allowScroll config to lock body scroll while a tour is active.
  • onDoneClick hook, fired when the done button on the final step is clicked. (#500)
  • data property on a step for passing arbitrary data, accessible from hooks for custom per-step logic. (#539)

Changed

  • Breaking - Custom popover footer buttons no longer get auto styled, style them using .driver-popover-footer button selector (#555)
  • The popover exposes driver-popover-side-* and driver-popover-align-* classes as per the rendered side and alignment nilbuild/driver.js#608
  • Arrow positioning has been fixed nilbuild/driver.js#558 nilbuild/driver.js#429 nilbuild/driver.js#406

Fixed

  • Document event listener leak in onDriverClick that left handlers attached after the tour was destroyed. (#452)
  • Dropped the CSS :has() selector for broader browser compatibility. (#586)
  • Positioned highlighted elements and their children (e.g. dropdown menus) no longer get clipped. (#454, #563)
  • removeChild DOMException when re-rendering a popover whose wrapper was already detached from the DOM. (#572)
  • Close button on a single-element highlight() popover did nothing when clicked. It now closes the popover by default, respecting allowClose and any custom onCloseClick. (#444)

1.5.0

  • Add done-btn class to next button (closes #610)
  • Pass final state to onDestroyed hook (closes #553)
  • Keep tour open on arrow-left at step 1 (closes #564)
  • Remove button text-shadow ghost text (closes #605)
  • Fire onNextClick on overlay nextStep (closes #576)
  • Add getNextStep to the driver API (closes #420)
  • Remove unicode characters from buttons
Commits
  • 565757b 1.6.0
  • 98d2c52 Fix close button not working on highlight
  • 0dfcdac Fix positioned highlighted elements get clipped #454 #563
  • 2633c2f Add test harness for multi-driver usage feature
  • 39f3377 Fix broken build and wrong x link
  • 4d9de02 feat: add 'data' property to DriveStep for custom logic support
  • 4637157 Add changelog page and refine docs styling
  • e1b0039 Style footer buttons via class so custom buttons can opt out
  • abc923b Make docs depend on unversioned package
  • 4062ddf Fix document event listener leak in onDriverClick (#452)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view
